senses.definition: lines of a document or drawing that are marked to be revised or corrected
examples: The editor sent back the contract with several redlines.
The architect reviewed the blueprints and noted the redlines.

senses.definition: to mark or revise a document or drawing with changes shown in red; to edit or revise
examples: The lawyer redlines the draft before submission.
They redline the contract to include new terms.

senses.definition: the act of marking up a document with edits in red; the process of applying redlines
examples: Redlining slowed the contract approval process.
Redlining is common in financial agreements.

senses.definition: plural of redlining; multiple instances of edits or markings in red on documents
examples: The redlinings on the draft caused several changes to the terms.
She reviewed the redlinings before sending the agreement.

senses.definition: in a way that is reddish or red; with a red appearance
examples: The sun turned the sky redly at dusk.
Her cheeks glowed redly after the workout.

senses.definition: a person with a red mouth; can be a nickname or descriptive term; not common in modern standard usage
examples: The old tale mentions a redmouth goblin.
In some regions, redmouth is used as a rough nickname.

senses.definition: a derogatory term for a rural working-class white person; can be used insultingly or reclaimed in some contexts
examples: The character in the novel wears the stereotype of a redneck.
Some communities reclaim the word redneck with humor.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling a redneck; rural or conservative in style or attitudes
examples: He drives a redneck pickup with loud exhaust.
Redneck humor can be blunt but affectionate.

senses.definition: having the appearance or characteristics of a redneck; rustic or unsophisticated
examples: A rednecked porch with a weathered chair stood in the yard.
They adopted a rednecked vibe for the comedy show.

senses.definition: plural of redneck; rural working-class people, often used pejoratively or humorously
examples: The comedy sketches stereotype rednecks.
Rednecks from the valley gathered for a barbecue.

senses.definition: the condition of being red or the color red; a red appearance
examples: Her cheeks showed a bright redness after the workout.
The skin’s redness indicated irritation.

senses.definition: exaggerated anger or anger expressed openly
examples: He spoke with a redness in his voice when recalling the event.
Her redness of anger surprised the audience.

senses.definition: plural of redness; multiple instances or kinds of redness
examples: The rednesses on the skin varied in intensity.
Different rednesses can indicate different causes.

senses.definition: to do something again, especially to correct a mistake or improve it
examples: I made a mistake, so I had to redo the assignment.
She decided to redo the kitchen with new cabinets.

senses.definition: an act of doing something again; a second attempt
examples: Give it a redo next time.
The redo was scheduled for Monday.

senses.definition: to connect again, especially to a dock or port; to return a vessel to a dock
examples: The ship will redock at the harbor for repairs.
They redocked the ferry after the storm.

senses.definition: past tense of redock; connected again to a dock or port
examples: The vessel redocked after the maintenance period.

senses.definition: to place again into a docket or docketing system; to register again in formal records
examples: They redocketed the case for a later date.
The filing was redocketed to the wrong category.

senses.definition: past tense of redocket; registered again in the docket
examples: The case was redocketed after the clerical error was found.

senses.definition: the act of placing a case again into the docket; the process of redocketing
examples: Redocketing can delay the hearing date.
There was a backlog of redocketing at the clerk's office.

senses.definition: the act of docking again or reattachment; often used in nautical or mechanical contexts
examples: Redocking of the shipment was completed successfully.
Redocking procedures require careful alignment.

senses.definition: third person singular present of redock; connects again to a dock or port
examples: The ship redocks at the port each morning.
They redocks the vessel after maintenance.

senses.definition: to document again; to provide new documentation
examples: The team redocumented the project with updated specifications.

senses.definition: past tense of re-do and did; not standard, likely error or playful coinage meaning 'did again'
examples: He redodid the task to meet the new requirements.

senses.definition: the act of doing something again; informal playful form of redoing
examples: The redodoing took longer than expected.

senses.definition: past participle of redo; completed again
examples: The task was redodone after the review.

senses.definition: third person singular of redo; to do again
examples: She redoes her presentation to improve clarity.
If the model fails, it redone to fix errors.

senses.definition: doing something again; in the process of being redone
examples: I am redoing the kitchen this weekend.
They are redoing the artwork for the exhibit.

senses.definition: a strong, pleasant, often evocative smell or fragrance; a distinctive aroma
examples: The redolence of pine filled the forest air.
Her kitchen exuded a redolence of garlic and herbs.
